questions: Item 2: The `calculateDuplicateFrame` function in `timelinePositionCalculator.ts` is imported by 6+ callers across the codebase (addImageToShotHelpers, useTimelineOrchestratorActions, timelineDropHelpers, etc.). Some of these may be for optimistic UI previews that legitimately need client-side frame calculation. Should we (a) only remove the function if ALL callers can be migrated to SQL-only, (b) keep it for non-RPC callers and just add clear documentation that SQL is authoritative, or (c) something else?

Item 4 (createShotWithFiles RPC): The file upload itself must stay client-side (Supabase Storage). The simpler fix is to have `uploadToShot` return complete generation metadata so we skip the re-fetch. The more robust fix is a new RPC that atomically creates the shot+links and returns hydrated data (like `create_shot_with_generations`). Which approach do you prefer, or should I assess both during Step 6 and pick the simpler one?

Item 3 (ClipAction snapshot): Three possible fixes — (A) make DynamicEffectRegistry skip notifications when data hasn't semantically changed, (B) split `data` dependency in TimelineEditor so registry changes don't trigger selectionShotCreationState recomputation, (C) use ref-stable callbacks. Option B is most surgical. Should I try B first, or do you have a preference?

success_criteria.criterion: All existing tests pass after each phase
TimelineCanvas shot group menu no longer closes on mousedown inside the portal (Phase 1)
No TypeScript collision resolution code serves as the authoritative source for DB writes — SQL RPCs are the authority for frame collision resolution (Phase 2)
ClipAction context menu does not flicker or lose items when EffectRegistry re-renders during an open menu (Phase 3)
shotActionsSnapshotRef workaround is fully removed from ClipAction.tsx (Phase 3)
createShotWithFilesPath makes at most one post-upload query to hydrate the cache, down from the current fetch of shot_generations (Phase 4)
calculateDuplicateFrame and ensureUniqueFrame remain available for legitimate client-side preview callers, with clear documentation that SQL is authoritative
Portal stopPropagation workaround has consistent comments across all three menus explaining the Node.contains() limitation
No new shared abstractions or utility files created — changes stay within existing files
Manual smoke test: create shot from file upload, verify correct images appear in gallery without page refresh
Manual smoke test: duplicate image in travel-between-images tool, verify frame placement is correct

assumptions: The `uploadToShot` mutation (via `processDroppedImages` / `useHandleExternalImageDrop`) already has access to generation metadata after upload — it creates the generation records. The second roundtrip exists because this data isn't surfaced to the caller, not because it's unavailable.

The travel-between-images `calculateDuplicateFrame` call in `useTimelineOrchestratorActions.ts:160` is for optimistic UI preview and passes `finalFrame` as `target_timeline_frame` to the RPC — meaning the SQL skips its own midpoint/+30 calculation. This makes the TS usage acceptable (preview) rather than authoritative (DB write).

The EffectRegistry re-render issue in Phase 3 is caused by `data` object reference instability in `TimelineEditor.tsx:234`, where `data` includes the registry whose version changes on every effect registration cycle. The fix should target memoization of the specific fields used by `selectionShotCreationState`, not the entire data object.

The three portal menus (ClipAction, SelectionContextMenu, TimelineCanvas) are the only portaled menus with custom click-outside handlers in the codebase. Other menus likely use Radix/shadcn which handles portal containment internally.
